name: White Blood Cell Count (WBC)
description: The total number of white blood cells in a volume of blood. It is a key indicator of immune system status, infection, inflammation, and bone marrow function.
interpretation: Leukocytosis (>11.0 x10³/µL in adults) may indicate infection, inflammation, tissue necrosis, or leukemia. Leukopenia (<4.5 x10³/µL) may result from bone marrow depression, severe infection, autoimmune disease, or certain medications.
warnings: Corticosteroids can cause a significant demargination leukocytosis.
Normal ranges vary significantly with age, especially in neonates and young children.
references: 1. Pagana KD, Pagana TJ. Mosby's Manual of Diagnostic and Laboratory Tests. 6th ed. St. Louis, MO: Elsevier; 2018.
examples: A 25-year-old female presenting with fever and right lower quadrant pain has a WBC of 16.5 x10³/µL, supporting a diagnosis of acute appendicitis.
notes: A differential count is usually required to determine which specific cell line is altered.
